rubric_focus: Only dialogue-confirmation commitments — things the speaker did not volunteer in prepared remarks but committed to after an analyst pushed for them through follow-up questions.

action_items.task: Add Mike Mayo's concern about artificial tangible-book-value targets / 'round-tripping' to the list of items to come back to.
action_items.evidence_quote: Very well. I'll add that to list. Next question.
dialogue_chain: Michael Mayo: [pushes back on the idea that managing to a tangible-book-value target produces good outcomes] ... I don't particularly like that because I think it puts you in an artificial position thinking that's always a good thing when it's not.
Jeremy Barnum: Very well. I'll add that to list. Next question.

action_items.task: Provide a clearer view on consumer-deposit fundamentals next quarter, once tax season is past.
action_items.evidence_quote: we'll be a little bit more confident in that, as you say, once we get through tax season. So maybe we'll know a little bit more next quarter.
dialogue_chain: Manan Gosalia: ... how you see deposit competition unfolding as similar smart tools become more widespread?
Jeremy Barnum: [discusses outlook] ... we'll be a little bit more confident in that, as you say, once we get through tax season. So maybe we'll know a little bit more next quarter.

action_items.task: Use JPMorgan's resources to find ways to reduce the firm's G-SIB charge under the new reproposal (regulatory arbitrage to serve clients).
action_items.evidence_quote: We will obviously use our brainpower to do something I don't like doing, which is trying to find a lot of ways to serve our clients properly and reduce the G-SIB charge, which is usually called arbitrage. So I'm not sure the outcome is great for the system, but we will find ways to do it.
dialogue_chain: Glenn Schorr / Michael Mayo: [push on big capital needs and how the G-SIB / Basel III reproposal disadvantages JPM]
James Dimon: I'll add one other thing. We will obviously use our brainpower... we will find ways to do it.

non_commitments.text: Reaffirmed 2026 guidance: NII ~$95.5B, adjusted expense ~$105B, card net charge-off rate ~3.4%.
non_commitments.why_not: Stated unilaterally by Jeremy in prepared remarks. Dimon explicitly disclaims the expense number as a commitment: 'The $105 billion is not a promise, it's an outcome of business results.'

non_commitments.text: Continued investment in JPM's new AI-powered savings tool.
non_commitments.why_not: Steven Chubak asked about it; Jeremy described existing strategy without committing to a specific new action or deadline.

non_commitments.text: Various 'we'll see' statements from Jeremy on macro / loan / deposit outlook.
non_commitments.why_not: Explicit signal of uncertainty; not a commitment.

non_commitments.text: $50 billion private-credit / leveraged-loan exposure number disclosed during Glenn Schorr's question.
non_commitments.why_not: Disclosure of a current state, not a commitment to act.

non_commitments.text: JPM continuing to 'be there for our clients for whatever they need.'
non_commitments.why_not: Aspirational positioning; not a specific action item.
